title: NPS vs EPF vs PPF - Which Retirement Savings is Best?
meta_description: NPS vs EPF vs PPF comparison 2026 - returns, tax benefits, withdrawal rules.

hero.one_liner: Three government-backed retirement savings - EPF for salaried (8.25%), PPF for everyone (7.1%), NPS for market-linked (9-12%).

sections.heading: Use All Three
content: If Salaried: EPF automatic (employer+employee). Open PPF 1.5L/year. Add NPS for extra 50K deduction. Total tax-saving: 2L/year.
If Self-Employed: No EPF. Open PPF (1.5L/year, 7.1%, tax-free) as primary. Add NPS for market-linked growth and 50K extra deduction.
Tax Optimization: EPF/PPF fill 1.5L 80C limit. NPS gives ADDITIONAL 50K under 80CCD(1B) - this is extra above 80C.

sections.heading: The NPS Annuity Problem
content: At age 60, you MUST use 40% of NPS corpus to buy annuity (monthly pension). Annuity rates poor - typically 5-6% return.
So 40% of savings earns below-inflation forever.
Remaining 60% withdrawn as lump sum (tax-free). You control it. This is good, but forced 40% annuity is why many experts rank NPS below PPF.
Workaround: Keep NPS to just 50K deduction benefit. Use extra money for PPF (100% your control) or equity mutual funds (higher returns, 100% flexible).

sections.heading: 25-Year-Old Salaried Strategy
content: EPF: 12% of salary. On 5L salary: 60K/year. Over 30 years at 8.25% = 75 lakh.
TOTAL RETIREMENT CORPUS: 3.75 crore. Tax saved: ~1.2 crore through deductions.

faqs.question: I am 25 and just started. What to prioritize?
faqs.answer: EPF (automatic), then PPF (500-1000/month), then NPS (only if maxed 80C). At 25 starting 5K/month across these grows to 1-2 crore by 55.

faqs.question: PPF rate decreasing. Still worth it?
faqs.answer: Yes. Even at 7.1%, best guaranteed + tax-free return available. After tax benefit adjustment, effective return is 9-10% for 30% tax bracket.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw EPF before retirement?
faqs.answer: Partial: housing, medical, marriage, education. Full: retirement (58), job loss 2+ months, emigration. Early full withdrawal taxable.

faqs.question: I am self-employed. EPF alternative?
faqs.answer: PPF is your alternative. Max 1.5L/year. Also consider Atal Pension Yojana for guaranteed 1000-5000/month pension after 60.

disclaimer: Return figures indicative. Rates change periodically.
